sourceRef: tip:Five signs your SAP process needs a refresh

slides.headline: Detect drift with five key signs
slides.body: Stay ahead of the curve and ensure your SAP process remains compliant and efficient.

slides.headline: COE forms not updated
slides.body: Review your Certificate of Eligibility forms. If the date on the form is from 2019 or earlier, it might be time to refresh.

slides.headline: Missing VA Form 22-1999
slides.body: Ensure you have the latest VA Form 22-1999 on file. This form is crucial for processing Yellow Ribbon applications.
bullets: Verify you have the current version.

slides.headline: Non-compliance with Chapter 33
slides.body: Check for any discrepancies in your Chapter 33 enrollment data. Ensure all students meet the eligibility requirements.

slides.headline: 85/15 rule not followed
slides.body: Ensure your SAP system reflects the 85/15 rule accurately. This rule governs the distribution of benefits.

slides.headline: Delayed updates
slides.body: Maintain a timeline for regular updates to your SAP system. Delays can lead to compliance issues.
Start reviewing your SAP process for signs of drift. Take the first step by scheduling a review.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Detect drift in your SAP process with these five key signs. Stay ahead of compliance requirements and ensure your system remains efficient. Schedule a review today.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Sign 5: TIMELY UPDATES. A top fix would be to add a visual icon or iconography to this slide to make it more engaging and easier to understand.
